original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: European Dermatology Forum in cooperation with European dermatology and pruritus experts
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: European Guideline on Chronic Pruritus: In cooperation with the European Dermatology Forum (EDF)
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: European international specialty guidance requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with chronic pruritus, including dermatologic, systemic, neurologic, psychiatric, drug-related, pregnancy, and older-person contexts.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary care, dermatology, and multidisciplinary assessment of chronic pruritus.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for a structured itch history, whole-skin examination, primary versus secondary lesion documentation, contextual review, and clinician-selected investigation documentation. It does not diagnose a cause or generate tests or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Section 5.1 — patient history and clinical examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, localisation, generalisation, time course, intensity, triggers, associated symptoms, medicines, exposures, personal and family history, whole skin, scalp, nails, oral and anogenital examination, and primary versus secondary lesions.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5.2–5.4 — diagnostic work-up and differential framework
exact_sections.locator: primary full text diagnostic-work-up sections and tables
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording laboratory or other results already available and clinician-selected investigation context according to history and examination; it does not generate a test panel.

title: European Guideline on Chronic Pruritus: In cooperation with the European Dermatology Forum (EDF)
publisher: European Dermatology Forum in cooperation with European dermatology and pruritus experts
jurisdiction: European international specialty guidance requiring UAE adaptation
population: Adults with chronic pruritus, including dermatologic, systemic, neurologic, psychiatric, drug-related, pregnancy, and older-person contexts.
intended_setting: Primary care, dermatology, and multidisciplinary assessment of chronic pruritus.
access_limitations: Official host returned a challenge or shell page rather than guideline content.
